results.title: Inferact
results.content: Today, we're proud to announce **Inferact**, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of vLLM, the most popular open-source LLM inference engine. Our mission is to grow vLLM as the world's AI inference engine and accelerate AI progress by making inference cheaper and faster. The capability gap between models and the systems that serve them is widening. Close the gap, and we unlock new possibilities. Today, deploying a frontier model at scale requires a dedicated infrastructure team. ## Why Us. vLLM sits at the intersection of models and hardware: a position that took years to build. When model vendors ship new architectures, they work with us to ensure day-zero support. When hardware vendors develop new silicon, they integrate with vLLM. ## Join Us. Through the open source community, we are fortunate to work with some of the best people we know. For Inferact, we're hiring engineers and researchers to work at the frontier of inference, where models meet hardware at scale.

results.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips | TechCrunch
results.content: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has released inference-performance software that allows a variety of open source large language models to run on a variety of chips — including Nvidia’s, AMD’s, Google’s TPU, Apple Metal, and Intel Arc. With ZML/LLMD, the newly launched LLM inference server, the company’s ambition is to break existing silos and make different chips available for AI use cases at their maximum available speed, and sometimes faster, ZML founder Steeve Morin told TechCrunch. But more than their region of origin, what matters to him is that ZML can work with them on “things that haven’t been done before anywhere in the world.”. He told TechCrunch that ZML has a good relationship with the AI chip giant, which has been gearing up for the rise of inference. He further credited ZML’s lean team of 20 people as the reason why the Paris-based startup has been able to move fast, with more releases in the plans.

results.title: AI inference provider Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B in funding - SiliconANGLE
results.content: ### AI inference provider Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B in funding. Baseten Inc., a startup with a platform for running artificial intelligence inference workloads, is raising $1.5 billion in funding. Baseten provides a platform that automates the workflow. The software is available as a managed service and as a standalone application that companies can deploy in their public cloud environments. Baseten’s platform is powered by three core modules the company calls inference engines. The first inference engine, BIS-LLM, is designed power large language models with a mixture of experts architecture. It’s optimized for dense LLMs, which are models that comprise a monolithic collection of artificial neurons rather than multiple neural networks. According to Baseten, the technology’s ability to switch providers is also handy when a company’s main public cloud has a shortage of graphics cards. As the parent company of SiliconANGLE, theCUBE Network, theCUBE Research, CUBE365, theCUBE AI and theCUBE SuperStudios — with flagship locations in Silicon Valley and the New York Stock Exchange — SiliconANGLE Media operates at the intersection of media, technology and AI.

results.title: LLM Inference Costs: What AI Founders Should Know
results.content: # LLM Inference: What AI Founders Should Know About Costs. Large language model (LLM) inference costs drive that gap, and getting a handle on them changes how you build, price and scale your product. This article covers the current pricing environment, the cost strategies that move the needle fastest and when to consider owning your own infrastructure. Model pricing has dropped quickly over the past 12 to 18 months, and founders who priced their products around earlier rate cards may find their cost assumptions outdated by the time they reach Series A conversations. Flagship models from Anthropic and OpenAI run at meaningfully higher rates, with output priced noticeably above input. Mistral Large input pricing has dropped from $3.00 per million tokens to $2.00 per million tokens. OpenAI cut o3 pricing by 80 percent in a single announcement, moving from $10.00 to $2.00 per million input tokens. Your product's output-to-input ratio determines more about your inference bill than which model you choose.

results.title: Inception – A new frontier in LLM speed
results.content: Mercury 2: the first reasoning model fast enough to pick up the phone. Mercury 2: the fastest reasoning model for voice agents. # A new frontier in LLMs speed. Inception’s breakthrough diffusion-based approach to language generation enables the world’s fastest, most efficient AI models with best-in-class quality. The Mercury diffusion models introduce blazing fast inference with frontier quality at a fraction of the cost of other top-tier models. ## The diffusion difference. Mercury diffusion LLMs (dLLMs) generate tokens in parallel, increasing speed and maximizing GPU efficiency. ## Build the future of AI apps with Mercury. Automate complex coding and other business workflows with with ultra-responsive AI. The fastest reasoning LLM and the first reasoning dLLM. We are excited to roll out Mercury to support all of our voice agents. Our models are OpenAI API compatible and a drop-in replacement for traditional LLMs. We’re available through major cloud providers like AWS Bedrock and Azure Foundry.

results.title: A Deep Dive on AI Inference Startups - by Kevin Zhang - East Wind
results.content: These companies range from model fine-tuning, to observability, to AI “abstraction” (e.g. AI inference as a service). The bet here is that as startups and enterprises add AI to their product offerings, they might be unwilling or unable to build these capabilities in-house, and would prefer a buy vs. In this post, I conduct a deep dive on AaaS (AI-as-a-Service) startups, specifically focusing on AI inference startups (see the red box below). * Why there’s even a need for AI inference abstraction. * What an investor needs to believe to invest in AI inference companies, focusing on the need for massive TAM expansion, product expansion, and potential M&A opportunities. ## Why is there a need for AI inference abstraction? One good way to understand the need for AI inference companies is to compare what companies needed to do if they want to deploy AI models (the world before), vs.
